goal: instantiate Claude Code build-continuity + supervised-resume system in the repo
plan: create .aios brain + CLAUDE.md + resume scripts + START_HERE, adapted to Windows/Python real state

failure_modes: auto-mode classifier blocked a folderOpen+ExecutionPolicy-Bypass task
fixes: made the VS Code task safe+manual; documented opt-in for auto-run rather than silently weakening guardrails
lessons: never auto-install a guardrail-weakening persistence hook; the project's own trust model forbids it. Trust the CODE for status (~75-80%), not the blueprint's 35%.

plan: backend approved_commands allowlist + human_required event reusing executor.execute_approved; minimal frontend resend-on-approve
actions: read executor.py (execute_approved confirmed)
locked design into RESUME.md before /compact
outcome: partial: design checkpointed, no code yet
lessons: checkpoint a multi-file plan into RESUME.md before a context compaction rather than half-applying it

goal: resumable in-chat YELLOW approval (chat pauses on a YELLOW command, then runs it after human approval)
plan: backend approved_commands allowlist + human_required pause reusing executor.execute_approved; map to the existing SSE human_required frame; minimal frontend resend-on-approve via an extracted streamGenerate
actions: tool_agent: __init__ approved_commands set; _format_exec_result helper; _execute returns new 'approval' status for an unauthorised YELLOW, or runs via execute_approved when whitelisted
run(): status=='approval' -> yield human_required + return (pause the turn)
main.py: GenerateRequest.approvedCommands; pass to ToolAgent; map agent human_required -> SSE human_required
tests: 3 tool_agent (pause / approved-runs / RED-refused-even-if-approved) + 2 api (pause / approved-runs)
frontend: approvedCommands state; extracted streamGenerate(history,approvedCmds); handleApproveAction whitelists + resumes the turn; handleSendMessage resets the whitelist
outcome: success: 94 passed, 1 skipped (was 89); eslint clean; vite build ok
lessons: reuse executor.execute_approved so human approval can never authorise RED (it still returns BLOCKED); resume a paused turn by replaying convHistory (which ends at the user msg, since the paused assistant turn records no answer) with the command whitelisted, rather than running it out-of-band via /api/terminal — this keeps the chat transcript coherent and lets the command's outcome still be reflected on

goal: fix two cosmetic glitches surfaced by the first live run of the 4h approval card
plan: clean the human-facing approval text + stop the perpetual tool_call spinner on a paused/settled turn
actions: live run showed the approval card, but the explanation displayed the raw classifier regex pattern (\bpip\s+install\b)
main.py human_required SSE now surfaces the command in plain language, never classify()'s raw reason (that pattern belongs in the audit log)
MessageBubble tool_call spinner was unconditional -> made it a static dot once the message is 'settled'
App.jsx marks the message settled:true on done/human_required/error
test_api asserts the friendly prompt is present AND the raw 'Caution operation requires approval' reason never leaks
outcome: success: 94 passed / 1 skipped; eslint+build clean; approval card now reads cleanly
failure_modes: unit tests asserted the human_required event fired but not the human-readability of its text, so the regex leak slipped through to the live UI
fixes: added a presentation-layer assertion (no raw classifier reason in the SSE body)
lessons: a passing 'event emitted' test is not a passing 'UI is readable' test; when surfacing an internal reason string to a human, assert on its content and keep regex/pattern internals in the audit log, not the prompt

goal: the approval card's Run/Reject buttons were clipped below the panel fold so the turn looked stuck at 'Awaiting approval'
plan: auto-scroll the chat panel when the approval card appears
actions: chatEndRef auto-scroll only fired on messages change, not when pendingAction (the tall approval card) appeared
added a pendingAction-triggered effect that scrolls chatEndRef into view after a 60ms layout delay
outcome: success: eslint+build clean; Run/Reject buttons now revealed when the card appears
failure_modes: a tall conditionally-rendered element (the approval card) appeared without an accompanying scroll, leaving its action buttons off-screen
fixes: scroll on the element's appearance, not only on list changes
lessons: when a new interactive element can push key controls below the fold, trigger a scroll on THAT element's mount, with a small delay so its layout is measured first; the user's 'still same issue' was a different problem (clipped buttons) than the cosmetics I'd just fixed — re-diagnose from the screenshot, don't assume

goal: the approval card's Run/Reject buttons were clipped below the fold inside the scrollable chat; auto-scroll did not reliably reveal them
plan: stop fighting scroll - pin the approval as a flex-shrink-0 action bar OUTSIDE the message scroll list, above the composer, so its controls are structurally always visible
actions: moved the pendingAction card out of the overflowY:auto messages div to a sibling between the list and the input composer
redesigned it premium: glassmorphism, slide-up entrance (approvalIn), ambient amber glow (approvalGlow), sweeping accent (sweep), gradient Run button with hover-lift + press feedback, YELLOW badge
added 3 keyframes to index.css; removed the now-pointless pendingAction scroll effect
outcome: success: eslint+build clean; approval controls can no longer be clipped regardless of scroll
failure_modes: two prior attempts (cosmetic text fix, then scroll-into-view) treated symptoms; the real fault was layout - a blocking control rendered inside a scroll region
fixes: render blocking/critical controls OUTSIDE scroll containers as pinned siblings
lessons: never place a required decision (approval) inside a scrollable log; pin it. When the user says 'still same problem' after a fix, the fix addressed the wrong layer - re-diagnose structurally instead of iterating on the same layer

goal: the chat UI was broken by a parallel premium-2026 rewrite (rewritten MessageBubble/LivePreview with incompatible props + new styles/*.css imported into App.jsx/index.css) that landed in the working tree alongside my edits
plan: per user choice (stabilize then polish): restore the working components + clean CSS baseline, park the new CSS unimported, then polish incrementally
actions: caught it because git status showed files I never edited (LivePreview, MessageBubble) modified + untracked styles/*; diffed them
my commit c500ed0 had unknowingly absorbed 3 foreign style-import lines because they were already in App.jsx/index.css when I edited those files

git restore MessageBubble+LivePreview to HEAD (working {msg} contract); git restore index.css to HEAD~1 (clean baseline, since the foreign edit also deleted 13 original lines) then re-applied my approval keyframes; removed the 3 foreign imports from App.jsx
left styles/{App,design-system,nexgen-3d,nexgen-layout}.css untracked+unimported to preserve the user work
outcome: success: eslint+build clean; app contract restored; pinned approval bar retained
failure_modes: staged files with git add <file> that contained concurrent foreign edits -> absorbed foreign import lines into my commit without noticing
fixes: always git diff <file> before git add when the tree may contain others concurrent edits; reconcile by restoring to a known-good ref + re-applying only my hunks
lessons: in a shared working tree treat every file as possibly co-edited: read the diff before staging, and prefer restoring to a known-good commit over surgically removing foreign lines when the foreign edit also deleted content

goal: add AWS Bedrock as a cloud LLM provider so the agentic loop can run in the cloud when the local 4GB GPU cannot host a model (Ollama CUDA-OOM)
plan: a BedrockClient implementing the same chat(messages,tools,model) contract as OllamaClient via the Converse API; provider routing in /api/generate; creds from env only; tested with a fake boto3 client
actions: config.py: BEDROCK_REGION/MODEL/MAX_TOKENS/ENABLED (env-driven; disabled unless region+model both set)

aios/core/bedrock.py: BedrockClient + _to_converse (pairs Ollama-style tool_calls/tool msgs into Converse toolUse/toolResult by minted ids) + _to_tool_config + _parse_output; lazy boto3 import; failures -> LLMError
main.py: get_bedrock_client dep + _select_chat_client routing (ollama.* -> local; else -> Bedrock on config.BEDROCK_MODEL when available, else local fallback); /api/generate uses the chosen client
tests: 8 in test_bedrock.py + 1 api routing test, all with injected fakes (no live AWS / no boto3 needed)
added boto3 stack to requirements via pip freeze
lessons: the agents Ollama-shaped protocol (tool_calls without ids; role:tool results) needs careful stateful conversion to Converse id-paired toolUse/toolResult; keep boto3 a lazy import so the cloud provider stays optional and tests need neither boto3 nor AWS creds. local-first stays default; cloud is opt-in via AIOS_BEDROCK_REGION + AIOS_BEDROCK_MODEL.

goal: let the dropdown run any Bedrock LLM (Claude/Nova/Llama/...) the account can invoke, not just one configured default
plan: backend list_models discovery + endpoint + route the selected id through to Converse; frontend Cloud (Bedrock) group from discovery with curated fallback
actions: bedrock.py: BedrockClient.list_models() via control-plane ListFoundationModels (TEXT, ON_DEMAND), dedup+sort, injectable ctrl_client for tests, []-on-error so a key without control-plane access degrades gracefully
main.py: /api/v1/models/bedrock endpoint; _select_chat_client now passes the selected model id through to Bedrock (each dropdown choice runs that model)
frontend: bedrockStatus probe + Cloud (Bedrock) group (falls back to the static curated list when discovery is empty) + PROVIDER_META entry
tests: +4 (list_models filter/sort/dedup + error; endpoint lists + empty)
failure_modes: pytest CRASHED with a Windows access-violation in torch/transformers — live app usage had populated the on-disk FAISS index, so /api/generate tests fell through _recall_memory -> hybrid_search and loaded the REAL sentence-transformers embedder (a model side-effect in a test path, which segfaults on this box)
fixes: stubbed aios.api.main.hybrid_search -> [] in the client fixture (recall tests override via the same monkeypatch); restores green AND isolates tests from live data, per the project rule of no model/network/shell side-effects in tests
lessons: when a project shares one DATA_DIR between the live app and tests, live usage can leak state into tests and trip code paths that were only safe while the index was empty; stub recall/model-loading in the fixture so tests never depend on on-disk state. ABSK Bedrock API keys may not grant control-plane (ListFoundationModels) access -> always provide a curated fallback for discovery.

edit_file schema sandbox-relative -> project-relative
tests monkeypatch PROJECT_ROOT in edit fixtures + new regression test

installed gh 2.93 via winget
gh repo create ai-editor --private --source=. --remote=origin --push

lessons: edit and read tools must share ONE path-addressing scheme (project-relative); a cloud-run fix is useless to a local-first app until it reaches the local tree - verify the commit is present before relying on it

goal: wire the existing Verifier into the live agentic loop (/api/generate) so the agent can PROVE its work, closing execute->verify->reflect
plan: add a `verify` tool to tool_agent.py routed to a reused Verifier built from the agent's own executor+on_failure; map VerifierResult to the loop (output,status,failed); do not rewrite verifier.py
actions: added verify entry to TOOL_SPECS (one `command` param) + module-docstring tool list
import Verifier; built self._verifier=Verifier(self.executor,on_failure=self.on_failure) ONCE in __init__
_dispatch routes verify -> _verify(command) calling .verify(command,session_id=self.session_id)
_verify maps BLOCKED->blocked (RED/out-of-scope refused, runner never called), pass/fail->ok with [VERIFY PASS|FAIL] N passed M failed (exit C) + summary
frontend: one additive TOOL_META verify entry in MessageBubble.jsx
added 3 tests: pass->no reflect, fail->reflects once (no double-reflect), RED->blocked & runner uncalled
outcome: success: +3 passing tests; tool_agent+verifier suites green (34/34); full suite 148 passed/4 skipped on Linux with 2 PRE-EXISTING environmental security failures (Windows-path + tmp-dir entropy), confirmed identical with my changes stashed
failure_modes: none from the change; env: no .venv/pytest preinstalled, torch wheel huge
fixes: ran in a throwaway venv with the ML training stack omitted (lazy-loaded; suite stubs the embedder) so faiss+numpy suffice
lessons: reuse the existing component's own reflection hook and gate the loop's reflect path by tool name (only execute_terminal reflects) to avoid double-reflection; a verify tool needs NO new event type - tool_result/tool_blocked already flow as `step` SSE, so frontend work is one TOOL_META line; before claiming a regression, stash and re-run to separate environmental failures from your own

frontend npm build clean + 9/9 vitest
read run(): reflection fires only inside name==execute_terminal so verify cannot double-reflect; verify routes through the gated Executor (RED refused, runner never called)
gh pr merge 1 --squash --delete-branch -> b6f26fb on master; pulled; branch deleted
re-ran suite on master: 153/1 green
outcome: success: Tier-1 #1 (wire Verifier) DONE and on master; AUDIT key-gap partially closed (Verifier now in the live execute->verify->reflect loop)
lessons: review cloud PRs on EVIDENCE - check out and run the suite on the real Windows baseline and read the integration point (run reflection gating) rather than trusting the description; GitHub reported CLEAN/MERGEABLE even though branch and master both edited RESUME (non-overlapping regions auto-merged)

goal: wire the Planner + 0.72 confidence gate into the live agentic loop (/api/generate) so the agent plans-before-acting (AUDIT Tier-1 #2), mirroring the merged verify tool
plan: add a `plan` tool to tool_agent.py backed by a reused Planner built from an INJECTED completion client; pass it from main.py via Depends(get_llm_client); advisory + fail-soft; do not rewrite planner.py/confidence_filter.py
actions: added plan entry to TOOL_SPECS (one `goal` param) + module-docstring tool list
import LLMClient + Planner/PlannerError; __init__ takes planner_llm: Optional[LLMClient], builds self._planner=Planner(planner_llm) once (None when absent)

_dispatch routes plan -> _plan(goal): None->[plan unavailable]; PlannerError->[plan error]; success->ordered steps w/ confidences + human-review section when requires_human; always status ok, failed False (advisory, never reflected)
main.py /api/generate: added planner_llm: LLMClient = Depends(get_llm_client) and passed planner_llm= into ToolAgent (NOT chat_client, which may be Bedrock)
frontend: one additive plan TOOL_META entry
added 4 tests + a FakePlannerLLM (.complete) fake: lists steps, flags <0.72 step, PlannerError clean (no reflect), graceful unavailable + done
outcome: success: +4 passing tests (45/45 tool_agent+verifier+planner); full backend 152 passed/4 skipped with the 2 PRE-EXISTING environmental test_security.py fails (confirmed identical with changes stashed); frontend vitest 9/9 + build + eslint green
failure_modes: first test cut filtered events by tool=='plan' only, catching the tool_call event before tool_result -> wrong event / KeyError on output
fixes: filter by type=='tool_result' (mirror the verify tests) before indexing output
lessons: CRITICAL: planner needs a COMPLETION client (.complete); the loop's chat client may be cloud Bedrock with no .complete() — inject the completion client, never reuse self.llm; advisory tools should surface bad input as a normal status-ok tool_result (NOT a security block, NOT a reflectable failure); when asserting on a tool's result event, filter by type=='tool_result' because both tool_call and tool_result carry the same `tool` name; squash-merged PRs leave the local feature branch off origin/master — reset --hard onto origin/master before the next increment so the new PR diff is clean

goal: review, patch, and merge PR #2 (wire the Planner + confidence gate into the live loop) under the supervised gate
plan: checkout branch, run full suite + frontend on Windows, read run() exception scope, patch the one robustness gap, then squash-merge
actions: gh pr checkout 2; backend 157 passed/1 skipped on Windows + frontend build/9-9; client-split verified (Planner uses injected get_llm_client, not self.llm)

FINDING in review: _plan caught only PlannerError but Planner.plan calls planner_llm.complete which raises LLMError if the local model is down; run() does not wrap _dispatch so that would abort the turn
patched _plan to also catch any Exception -> graceful [plan error]; +1 regression test (planner LLM raises LLMError -> clean result, loop done, no reflect); 158 passed
committed 0876c29 on the branch + pushed; gh pr merge 2 --squash --delete-branch -> 3b71461 on master; pulled; re-ran 158/1 green
outcome: success: AUDIT Tier-1 #2 (wire Planner) DONE on master; integration gap CLOSED - Verifier + Planner both in the live loop (plan-before-act + verify-after); runway to the Self-Analysis module is clear
fixes: the LLMError robustness gap caught in review and patched before merge
lessons: the FIRST loop tool that makes an LLM call inside _dispatch needs its own broad exception guard - run() only wraps the chat call, not _dispatch; an advisory tool must catch all failures and degrade, never propagate; reviewing on evidence (reading run() try/except scope) caught a gap the PR claimed was covered

goal: clear AUDIT Tier-2 #3 (rollback git repo lived inside the main-repo-tracked training_ground/) and #4 (tests ran against the live data/ DBs+FAISS index) in ONE focused PR, as two separable changes
plan: .aios/state/AUDIT.md Tier-2 + RESUME next-action: #3 relocate the rollback git DATABASE under a new gitignored config.ROLLBACK_DIR (separate_git_dir) so the sandbox stays the work-tree but no .git DB lands in-tree; gitignore the residual pointer + .gitkeep. #4 add tests/conftest.py that sets AIOS_DATA_DIR to a tempfile.mkdtemp at module level BEFORE aios.config imports, isolating all derived paths; the empty temp index lets hybrid_search short-circuit so the client-fixture stub could be dropped.
actions: added config.ROLLBACK_DIR (=_env_path(AIOS_ROLLBACK_DIR, DATA_DIR/'rollback')) + __all__

RollbackEngine: added git_dir param; default sandbox uses Repo.init(work_tree, separate_git_dir=ROLLBACK_DIR); injected repo_dir keeps DB in-tree; _ensure_repo treats a .git pointer FILE or dir as 'already init' and reopens via work-tree
gitignored training_ground/.git + training_ground/.gitkeep
added tests/conftest.py (module-level AIOS_DATA_DIR tempdir + atexit cleanup)
removed the now-unneeded hybrid_search stub from tests/test_api.py client fixture
added tests/test_data_isolation.py (3) + 3 rollback tests
verified production path: training_ground/.git is a 43-byte pointer, real DB under data/rollback/, git status clean (git check-ignore confirms)
ran full suite + stashed-diff comparison for the 2 pre-existing security fails
outcome: success: full suite 159 passed / 4 skipped / 2 failed; the 2 fails are the pre-existing+environmental test_security.py cases (verified identical with changes stashed). Rollback snapshot/rollback + project-root refusal intact; live data/ untouched.
lessons: GitPython separate_git_dir cleanly moves a git DB out of a tracked work-tree, leaving only a small 'gitdir:' POINTER file; treat either a .git dir OR a .git pointer file as 'repo already initialised' and always reopen via the work-tree path so the pointer is followed transparently. For test isolation of import-time-derived config paths, set the env var at conftest.py MODULE level (pytest imports conftest before test modules, hence before the first aios.config import) rather than in a fixture. An isolated empty FAISS index makes hybrid_search short-circuit without loading the embedder, which let a long-standing test stub be removed and the no-model-in-tests contract be pinned by an explicit test instead.
